skipped.heading: 实施记录
skipped.reason: implementation logistics: file paths, smoke names, commits

skipped.heading: 后果
skipped.reason: rationale summaries; durable truths captured in mechanism entries

drafts.title: Project ID is the sole portable identity; path and git remote are not
drafts.compiledTruth: In pi-astack project binding, project_id is the only identity carrier for a project. Filesystem paths (cwd) and git remotes are explicitly rejected as identity signals: paths can move, remotes can rename, fork, mirror, or switch SSH to HTTPS. The .abrain-project.json manifest stores only schema_version and project_id — no path, no remote, no machine_id, no created_at. This ensures project identity is stable across directory moves, re-clones, and machine changes. Resolution via cwd prefix or git remote is permanently excluded by design, not just discouraged.

drafts.title: Bound status requires manifest + registry + local-map all satisfied simultaneously
drafts.compiledTruth: Bound status requires three simultaneous conditions: (1) .abrain-project.json in the project repo — portable identity claim, user decides whether to commit, contains only project_id; (2) ~/.abrain/projects/<project_id>/_project.json — abrain-side git-tracked project existence proof; (3) current path present in ~/.abrain/.state/projects/local-map.json — machine-local path authorization, NOT git-tracked. Missing any one causes rejection of all project-scoped writes. This triple-gating prevents a forged or copied manifest from granting vault or sediment access without the user having explicitly run /abrain bind on that machine.

drafts.title: Migration commands must not create or determine project identity
drafts.compiledTruth: /memory migrate does not accept --project=<id> and never creates project identity. Identity must be established first via explicit /abrain bind --project=<id>; migration then reads project_id from the active binding. The correct flow is: /abrain bind --project=<id> -> /memory migrate --dry-run -> /memory migrate --go. Passing --project= to migrate is rejected with an explicit error. If the project is not bound, migration refuses with the current binding status. Principle: migration is data movement, not identity decision. This separation was introduced because accepting --project= inline caused typos to be silently fixated as real project directories on --go.

drafts.title: Active project state machine: 7 states, only bound permits project-scoped writes
drafts.compiledTruth: pi resolves active project at boot from the cwd git root; bash cd does not change the active project (boot-time snapshot). The state machine has 7 states: bound (project-scoped writes allowed), manifest_missing, manifest_invalid, registry_missing, registry_mismatch, path_unconfirmed, path_conflict. Any non-bound state rejects: /memory migrate --go, sediment project-scoped writes (create/update/merge/archive/supersede/delete), and project vault ops. Always-allowed regardless of binding: read-only memory ops (search/get/list/neighbors), /memory lint, doctor-lite, and global vault. pi does not show a blocking interactive prompt at startup for unbound state; it enters degraded mode silently and waits for /abrain bind.

drafts.title: local-map is machine-local path authorization, not git-tracked, multi-path per project
drafts.compiledTruth: ~/.abrain/.state/projects/local-map.json maps project_id to a list of confirmed local paths and is deliberately NOT git-tracked (under gitignored ~/.abrain/.state/). It supports multiple paths per project: multiple clones, git worktrees, temp checkouts, old and new paths coexisting. Each entry carries first_seen, last_seen, confirmed_at; updating an existing path updates last_seen without duplicating. Path presence here is the machine-authorization confirmation step — this is what prevents a malicious repo from gaining project vault or sediment access by forging .abrain-project.json, since local-map is only written when the user explicitly runs /abrain bind on that machine.

drafts.title: All project-scoped sediment mutation APIs check binding before writing and audit rejects
drafts.compiledTruth: Every project-scoped sediment mutation API (writeProjectEntry, updateProjectEntry, deleteProjectEntry, and derived merge/archive/supersede) checks binding status before writing. If not bound, the operation is rejected and an audit row is emitted: {operation: reject, reason: project_not_bound, binding_status, hint: /abrain bind}. This guard is a universal precondition for all project-scoped writers. After B5 writer cutover writers route to <abrainHome>/projects/<projectId>/ and never write to .pensieve/ for new entries, but the strict binding guard remains active. Audit rows preserve forensic trace even on rejected writes.

drafts.title: Project vault access requires bound status; manifest alone is insufficient
drafts.compiledTruth: Project vault operations — vault_release(scope=project), /secret set/list/forget default scope, $PVAULT_*, $VAULT_* project lookup — all require active project status = bound. A forged or copied .abrain-project.json in a malicious repo cannot grant project vault access: the local-map must contain a user-confirmed path on that machine. Defense principle: manifest is identity claim, local-map is machine authorization. When not bound: project vault ops are rejected; global vault (--global / $GVAULT_*) still works; $VAULT_* falls back to global or is rejected with a message stating project scope is unavailable.

drafts.title: /abrain bind is idempotent for same project; rejects path already owned by another project
drafts.compiledTruth: /abrain bind idempotency rules: (a) manifest exists + --project matches existing project_id -> allowed, update registry and local-map; (b) manifest exists + --project differs -> rejected, rename/repair is a separate flow; (c) registry exists + project_id matches -> allowed, update updated_at; (d) current path already in local-map for a different project_id -> rejected, fail-loud; repair requires explicit /abrain unbind-path or /abrain repair. Rule (d) prevents a path from being silently re-bound to a different project, which would misdirect that project's vault and sediment writes.

drafts.title: No --local-only mode; /abrain bind always writes .abrain-project.json to the repo
drafts.compiledTruth: /abrain bind always writes .abrain-project.json to the project repository root. There is no --local-only flag or local-only binding mode. Whether to commit that file to version control is the user's own choice: if not committed, a new clone or new machine requires the user to copy or re-run /abrain bind. This design keeps the binding mechanism uniform with one code path and no mode switch. The manifest is a portable identity claim, not a secret; committing it enables project identity to travel with the repo across machines and collaborators.
